ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Jul-16-2015 Continued To Aug-26-15 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Motion And Motion Of Defendants For An Order To Transfer Venue To Napa County
Off Calendar Jul-02-2015 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances in the matter. Ruling: The Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: DEFENDANTS NALIN MODY AND MEERA MODY'S Motion For An Order To Transfer Venue To Napa County is denied. The burden is on the moving party to make a prima facie showing that plaintiff's venue selection is not proper under any statutory grounds. (Mitchell v. Sup. Ct. (1986) 186 Cal.App.3d 1040, 1046.) Defendants have not met their initial burden because they have not submitted any admissible evidence. The Declaration of Nalin Mody and the Declaration of Meera Mody are declared under penalty of perjury under the laws of the state of Hawaii. In Kulshrestha v. First Union Comm. Corp. (2004) 33 Cal.4th 601, the Cal. Supreme Court discusses CCP sec. 2015.5, which provides that if a declaration is executed outside of California, the declaration must state that it is "declared under the laws of the State of California." The Court held that substantial compliance with the statute is insufficient, stating "[i]t seems clear that out-of-state declarations offend section 2015.5, and are not deemed sufficiently reliable for purposes of that statute, unless they follow its literal terms." (Id. at 611.) The Court concluded that an out-of-state declaration that does not explicitly state that it is executed under the laws of the State of California "cannot be used as evidence." (Id. at 618.). Order signed. Judge Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk Felicia Green; Dept. 302. (302/EHG)

Notice Of Motion And Motion Of Defendants For An Order To Transfer Venue To Napa County
